question: For each region, which product generated the most revenue? Give the region name, the product name, and that revenue.
gold_sql: WITH r AS (SELECT reg.name AS region, p.name AS product, SUM(p.price*o.quantity) AS rev, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY reg.name ORDER BY SUM(p.price*o.quantity) DESC) rk FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id=p.id JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.id JOIN regions reg ON c.region_id=reg.id GROUP BY reg.name, p.name) SELECT region, product, rev FROM r WHERE rk=1

question: Which customers placed orders in at least 4 different months? Give their names.
gold_sql: SELECT c.name FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.id GROUP BY c.id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT substr(o.order_date,1,7)) >= 4

question: List each product whose total revenue is above the average total revenue across all ordered products.
gold_sql: WITH pr AS (SELECT p.id, p.name, SUM(p.price*o.quantity) rev FROM products p JOIN orders o ON o.product_id=p.id GROUP BY p.id) SELECT name FROM pr WHERE rev > (SELECT AVG(rev) FROM pr)

question: For each category, what percentage of total company revenue does it represent? Give the category and the percentage rounded to one decimal place.
gold_sql: SELECT p.category, ROUND(100.0*SUM(p.price*o.quantity)/(SELECT SUM(p2.price*o2.quantity) FROM orders o2 JOIN products p2 ON o2.product_id=p2.id),1) FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id=p.id GROUP BY p.category

question: Name the customers who have never ordered an Electronics product.
gold_sql: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT o.customer_id FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id=p.id WHERE p.category='Electronics')

question: What is the second most expensive product in each category? Give the category and the product name.
gold_sql: WITH r AS (SELECT category, name, price, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY price DESC) rk FROM products) SELECT category, name FROM r WHERE rk=2

question: Which region has the highest average order value (revenue per order)? Give the region name and that average, rounded to 2 decimal places.
gold_sql: SELECT reg.name, ROUND(AVG(p.price*o.quantity),2) av FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id=p.id JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.id JOIN regions reg ON c.region_id=reg.id GROUP BY reg.name ORDER BY av DESC LIMIT 1

question: Show the total revenue for each month (YYYY-MM), ordered chronologically.
gold_sql: SELECT substr(o.order_date,1,7) mo, SUM(p.price*o.quantity) rev FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id=p.id GROUP BY mo ORDER BY mo

question: Which products were ordered by customers from more than one region? Give the product names.
gold_sql: SELECT p.name FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id=p.id JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.id GROUP BY p.id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.region_id) > 1

question: For the top 3 customers by total spend, give the customer name and total spend, highest first.
gold_sql: SELECT c.name, SUM(p.price*o.quantity) spend FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id=p.id JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.id GROUP BY c.id ORDER BY spend DESC LIMIT 3

question: Which customers spent more than the average customer's total spend? Give their names.
gold_sql: WITH cs AS (SELECT c.id, c.name, SUM(p.price*o.quantity) spend FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id=c.id JOIN products p ON o.product_id=p.id GROUP BY c.id) SELECT name FROM cs WHERE spend > (SELECT AVG(spend) FROM cs)

question: What is the running cumulative revenue after each month? Give the month and the cumulative revenue, chronologically.
gold_sql: WITH m AS (SELECT substr(o.order_date,1,7) mo, SUM(p.price*o.quantity) rev FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id=p.id GROUP BY mo) SELECT mo, SUM(rev) OVER (ORDER BY mo) FROM m ORDER BY mo

question: How many distinct customers ordered the single best-selling product by total quantity?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) FROM orders WHERE product_id = (SELECT product_id FROM orders GROUP BY product_id ORDER BY SUM(quantity) DESC LIMIT 1)

question: For each region, how many distinct customers placed at least one order? Give the region name and the count, highest first.
gold_sql: SELECT reg.name, COUNT(DISTINCT o.customer_id) n FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.id JOIN regions reg ON c.region_id=reg.id GROUP BY reg.name ORDER BY n DESC
